maintenance.description: Planned maintenance windows. Shown on the status page; affected monitors are not probed while a window is active.

notification_templates.description: Optional custom notification text templates by event type. Placeholders: {monitor}, {count}, {error}, {status}.

title.description: Short headline shown on the status page banner. Must be unique (used to derive the window id).

body.description: Optional longer description of the planned work.

monitors.description: Names of affected monitors. Omit for a global window (all monitors).

deploy_config.description: Deployment inputs. provision creates the D1 database and queue by name; their IDs are resolved by name at deploy time and never stored here.

name.description: Worker name. Resource names derive from it: <name>-prod-db, <name>-notifications.

domain.description: Custom domain route, e.g. status.example.com. Omit to serve on workers.dev only.

vpc_config.description: Cloudflare Workers VPC bindings (beta) for mode: internal monitors. Consumes pre-existing VPC resources by id — heartbeatflare never provisions Networks/Services/Tunnels. Resource ids should be ${VAR} placeholders resolved from .env / CI env at wrangler-generation time.

networks.description: Tunnel-backed VPC Networks. Cloudflare Mesh (network_id: cf1:network) is unsupported in v1.

binding.description: Worker binding name referenced by a monitor's vpc_binding. Unique across networks and services.

remote.description: Use the remote resource during local dev. Harmless for production deploys.

services.description: Scoped VPC Services. The destination host:port is fixed by service_id; a monitor's target host:port is ignored for routing (URL only sets Host/SNI).

binding.description: Worker binding name referenced by a monitor's vpc_binding. Unique across networks and services.

remote.description: Use the remote resource during local dev. Harmless for production deploys.

auth_config.description: Optional Cloudflare Access authentication for /private. When present, unauthenticated users see only public monitors. Scope the Access application to the /private path (Destination <host>/private), not the bare hostname — otherwise Access walls off the public page too.

team_name.description: Your Cloudflare Zero Trust team name (the label before .cloudflareaccess.com, e.g. "acme" for acme.cloudflareaccess.com — not the full domain). Supports ${VAR} substitution.

aud.description: Application AUD tag from CF Access dashboard. Supports ${VAR} substitution.

channel.description: Channel name for display purposes, e.g. "#alerts".

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers. Values support ${VAR} substitution.

to.description: Recipient email address or list of addresses.

bot_token.description: Telegram Bot API token. Supports ${VAR} substitution.

chat_id.description: Telegram chat or channel ID. Supports ${VAR} substitution.

monitor.description: A monitor. `target` is required for all types except `heartbeat` (push); a heartbeat monitor is identified by its URL id and validated by a Cloudflare Worker Secret derived from its name.

target.description: URL, host:port, or domain to monitor.

interval.description: Check interval (heartbeat: expected beat period), minimum 60s. Examples: "60s", "5m", "300s", "6h", "1d". Defaults to 5m.

enabled.description: Set to false to pause this monitor: still visible on the status page but not probed.

notification_channels.description: Names of notification channels for this monitor. If omitted, falls back to is_default channels.

headers.description: Custom HTTP request headers (type: http only). Values support ${VAR} substitution from Worker secrets. The default User-Agent (heartbeatflare/1.0) cannot be overridden.

vpc_binding.description: Required for mode: internal monitors (type http or tcp): the name of a deploy.vpc network/service binding to probe through. Must not be set on mode: external monitors. mode: internal does not support type dns or ssl in v1.

cooldown.description: Minimum time between repeated alerts, e.g. "300s" or "5m".

escalation.description: Re-notify interval while an incident stays open, e.g. "1800s", "30m" or "1h". Omit to disable escalation.
